summary: Phase 14 frontend e2e via Playwright. Validated salon admin login, /salon/inventory page (17 pre-seeded items rendered as cards with full action set), full CRUD (create UI_TEST_ITEM_19722, edit price 199->249, sell qty=2 cash POS), Movements tab (53 rows, filter to sale_to_customer narrowed to 6), per-item Movement modal (2 entries: manual_add + sale_to_customer), low-stock filter toggle, Orders nav, and the new Payment-mode change flow on a real COD/delivered order (#84786852): COD → UPI confirmed in Payment card + Change Log entry + Status timeline entry; same-mode no-op returns 'Payment mode unchanged' toast. Marketplace regression PASS. 11/12 flows green; 1 minor menu-toggle test selector miss (sidebar/hamburger drawer didn't expose Inventory text — code is correct, direct route works).

ui_bugs.issue: Same-mode submission (e.g. UPI -> UPI) still triggers onSaved() which closes the modal, even though backend returns 'Payment mode unchanged' and no new history entry is added. UX-wise the user may expect the modal to stay open so they can pick a different mode. Minor; backend behaviour is correct (validated, history unchanged).

issues: Sidebar drawer not auto-visible on desktop (1920x1080); requires hamburger toggle. Once toggled, 'Inventory' menu entry is correctly present in code (EnhancedSalonDashboard.js:990) — but in our Playwright test the first header button (hamburger) toggle did not surface the menu items in the snapshot. Likely a sidebar component that renders the drawer outside the immediate DOM tree on click. Manually-verifiable: direct navigation to /salon/inventory works and the rename is reflected; old label 'My Store Orders' is absent from page source. Recommend adding a data-testid='nav-inventory' on the menu entry for easier QA.

action_items: Consider keeping PaymentModeModal open on same-mode no-op (do not call onSaved() when r.data.message indicates 'unchanged') so user can correct selection.
Add a data-testid='nav-inventory' (and other sidebar menu items) in EnhancedSalonDashboard.js around line 990 for robust test selectors.
Optional: emit a distinct toast variant (info, not success) when payment mode is unchanged.
critical_code_review_comments: SalonInventoryPage.js (715 lines) is borderline of the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting CreateItemModal, ActionModal, ItemMovementsModal, MovementsTab into /pages/salon/inventory/*.jsx for maintainability.

SalonInventoryPage authHeaders useMemo dependency is empty [] which is correct for snapshot semantics, but if the token is rotated mid-session the page will keep using the stale token until full reload. Consider listening to a storage event.
PaymentModeModal calls onSaved() unconditionally on 2xx — should branch on r.data.changed or similar to avoid closing on no-op.
Inventory cards: 'Reserved' stat sums qty_reserved_for_internal + qty_reserved_for_customer_orders into a single number, which hides the breakdown. Tooltip would help branch managers.
test_credentials: Salon admin identifier=admin password=salon123 (salon_id 459ff694-c246-4891-a535-fc8f23b58ac3) — validated. Order 84786852-0a70-4b61-9340-9109c4ae2008 used for payment-mode change (now reads UPI).
seed_data_creation: Created salon inventory item 'UI_TEST_ITEM_19722' (id 65476f9f-dd83-45bc-b435-64d782c3f71a) with selling_price=249 after edit, qty_total=8 after POS sale of 2. One manual_add and one sale_to_customer movement row added. Order 84786852 payment_mode flipped cod->upi with note 'Paid via UPI at delivery'; financials should mirror this. Safe to clean up if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Phase 14 inventory CRUD/lifecycle UI flows and the Payment-mode change flow are wired and working e2e against backend. Use data-testid hooks documented in the review request — they all exist except the menu items in EnhancedSalonDashboard.js (recommend adding nav-* testids). Pre-seeded test order 84786852 is now payment_mode=upi with one history entry; rerunning the test will flip it back to cod via the cash branch. Direct nav URL https://staff-form-fix.preview.emergentagent.com/salon/inventory works for any test that struggles with the hamburger toggle.
rca of the issue: Menu-visibility test failure RCA: Playwright clicked the first <button> in <header> hoping to open a drawer. The salon dashboard appears to use a different sidebar pattern (likely a slide-in component rendered via portal) — so simulating a click on the hamburger doesn't immediately expose menu text in get_by_text(). However, page source DOES contain 'Inventory' menu definition (verified by grep on EnhancedSalonDashboard.js:990) and 'My Store Orders' is absent from rendered DOM, so the rename is verified. Mitigation: add data-testid='nav-inventory' to the menu entry; this would let the test pass deterministically without depending on the drawer being open.
